title: eriophyllum confertiflorum var. tridactylum
intro: Eriophyllum confertiflorum var. tridactylum, also known as Tridactyl Woolly Sunflower, is a perennial herb native to the southwestern United States and Baja California. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating skin conditions such as wounds, eczema, and acne. The health benefits of Eriophyllum confertiflorum var. tridactylum include its ability to reduce pain and inflammation, making it a potential remedy for arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
